title: Who Was I Before the Baby, and Where Is She
subtitle: A journal prompt set for naming the pre-baby self and tracing the threads of her into now
excerpt: A journal prompt set for the woman who was here before the baby. The prompts open wide and close in. Used in the first three years of new motherhood, or any time the pre-baby self feels far away.
sourceNotes: Original to homemade.education. The pre-and-post identity-mapping shape is common across journal work in any major identity passage.

practice.whenToUse: Use in the first three years of new motherhood. Also good at any later point when the pre-baby self feels far away.

content.text: A journal prompt set for the woman who was here before the baby. Six prompts, total time about ten minutes. The first two open wide. The last four close in on the threads of her that still run through today.
New to journal prompts as a practice? Read
journal prompts as practice
first.
The practice
Warm-up
Who was I, the morning before I found out?
Write whatever comes. Where you were, what you were doing, what you were planning that week. Two or three sentences.
What did she love that I have not done in a while?
Write a list of three things. They can be small. A weekly thing, a seasonal thing, a thing that was a habit then but is not now.
Closer in
What of her is still here, in the way I mother?
Look for the bits of her that survived: the music she liked, the way she spoke, what made her laugh, what she put up with and what she did not.
What of her went under, that I would like back?
Name one. Be specific. "My reading habit." "Long baths." "A sharp opinion about the news." "Friends I would ring on a Tuesday."
What is the smallest version of that I could do this week?
Not the whole habit. The smallest version. "Ten pages of a book." "Bath on Saturday after bath-time." "Read one news piece and tell my friend." "Ring one friend on Tuesday."
What will I do today, before the day ends, to show her that she is still here?
One small move. Write it down. Then close the journal and do the thing.
Where this practice comes from
Original to homemade.education. The before-and-after journal shape is used in many books on big life changes.
